speaker: CHIRON
text: Demetrius, here’s the son of Lucius.

speaker: CHIRON
text: He hath some message to deliver us.

speaker: AARON
text: Ay, some mad message from his mad grandfather.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: My lords, with all the humbleness I may,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: I greet your Honors from Andronicus—

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              And pray the Roman gods confound you both.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Gramercy, lovely Lucius. What’s the news?

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: That you are both deciphered, that’s the news,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: For villains marked with rape.—May it please you,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: My grandsire, well advised, hath sent by me

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: The goodliest weapons of his armory

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: To gratify your honorable youth,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: The hope of Rome; for so he bid me say,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: And so I do, and with his gifts present

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: Your Lordships, that, whenever you have need,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text: You may be armèd and appointed well,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              LUCIUS
text:
And so I leave you both—
                (
                aside
                )
                 
              like bloody villains.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: What’s here? A scroll, and written round about.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Let’s see:

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: “Integer vitae, scelerisque purus,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Non eget Mauri iaculis, nec arcu.”

speaker: CHIRON
text: O, ’tis a verse in Horace; I know it well.

speaker: CHIRON
text: I read it in the grammar long ago.

speaker: AARON
text: Ay, just; a verse in Horace; right, you have it.

speaker: AARON
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              Now, what a thing it is to be an ass!

speaker: AARON
text: Here’s no sound jest. The old man hath found their guilt

speaker: AARON
text: And sends them weapons wrapped about with lines

speaker: AARON
text: That wound, beyond their feeling, to the quick.

speaker: AARON
text: But were our witty empress well afoot,

speaker: AARON
text: She would applaud Andronicus’ conceit.

speaker: AARON
text: But let her rest in her unrest awhile.—

speaker: AARON
text: And now, young lords, was ’t not a happy star

speaker: AARON
text: Led us to Rome, strangers, and, more than so,

speaker: AARON
text: Captives, to be advancèd to this height?

speaker: AARON
text: It did me good before the palace gate

speaker: AARON
text: To brave the tribune in his brother’s hearing.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: But me more good to see so great a lord

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Basely insinuate and send us gifts.

speaker: AARON
text: Had he not reason, Lord Demetrius?

speaker: AARON
text: Did you not use his daughter very friendly?

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: I would we had a thousand Roman dames

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: At such a bay, by turn to serve our lust.

speaker: CHIRON
text: A charitable wish, and full of love!

speaker: AARON
text: Here lacks but your mother for to say amen.

speaker: CHIRON
text: And that would she, for twenty thousand more.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Come, let us go and pray to all the gods

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: For our belovèd mother in her pains.

speaker: AARON
text: Pray to the devils; the gods have given us over.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Why do the Emperor’s trumpets flourish thus?

speaker: CHIRON
text: Belike for joy the Emperor hath a son.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Soft, who comes here?

speaker: NURSE
text: Good morrow, lords.

speaker: NURSE
text: O, tell me, did you see Aaron the Moor?

speaker: AARON
text: Well, more or less, or ne’er a whit at all,

speaker: AARON
text: Here Aaron is. And what with Aaron now?

speaker: NURSE
text: O, gentle Aaron, we are all undone!

speaker: NURSE
text: Now help, or woe betide thee evermore.

speaker: AARON
text: Why, what a caterwauling dost thou keep!

speaker: AARON
text: What dost thou wrap and fumble in thy arms?

speaker: NURSE
text: O, that which I would hide from heaven’s eye,

speaker: NURSE
text: Our empress’ shame and stately Rome’s disgrace.

speaker: NURSE
text: She is delivered, lords, she is delivered.

speaker: AARON
text: To whom?

speaker: NURSE
text: I mean, she is brought abed.

speaker: AARON
text: Well, God give her good rest. What hath he sent her?

speaker: NURSE
text: A devil.

speaker: AARON
text: Why, then she is the devil’s dam. A joyful issue!

speaker: NURSE
text: A joyless, dismal, black, and sorrowful issue!

speaker: NURSE
text: Here is the babe, as loathsome as a toad

speaker: NURSE
text: Amongst the fair-faced breeders of our clime.

speaker: NURSE
text: The Empress sends it thee, thy stamp, thy seal,

speaker: NURSE
text: And bids thee christen it with thy dagger’s point.

speaker: AARON
text: Zounds, you whore, is black so base a hue?

speaker: AARON
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                baby
                .
                 
              Sweet blowse, you are a beauteous blossom, sure.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Villain, what hast thou done?

speaker: AARON
text: That which thou canst not undo.

speaker: CHIRON
text: Thou hast undone our mother.

speaker: AARON
text: Villain, I have done thy mother.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And therein, hellish dog, thou hast undone her.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Woe to her chance, and damned her loathèd choice!

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Accursed the offspring of so foul a fiend!

speaker: CHIRON
text: It shall not live.

speaker: AARON
text: It shall not die.

speaker: NURSE
text: Aaron, it must. The mother wills it so.

speaker: AARON
text: What, must it, nurse? Then let no man but I

speaker: AARON
text: Do execution on my flesh and blood.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: I’ll broach the tadpole on my rapier’s point.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Nurse, give it me. My sword shall soon dispatch it.

speaker: AARON
text: Sooner this sword shall plow thy bowels up!

speaker: AARON
text: Stay, murderous villains, will you kill your brother?

speaker: AARON
text: Now, by the burning tapers of the sky

speaker: AARON
text: That shone so brightly when this boy was got,

speaker: AARON
text: He dies upon my scimitar’s sharp point

speaker: AARON
text: That touches this my firstborn son and heir.

speaker: AARON
text: I tell you, younglings, not Enceladus

speaker: AARON
text: With all his threat’ning band of Typhon’s brood,

speaker: AARON
text: Nor great Alcides, nor the god of war

speaker: AARON
text: Shall seize this prey out of his father’s hands.

speaker: AARON
text: What, what, you sanguine, shallow-hearted boys,

speaker: AARON
text: You white-limed walls, you alehouse painted signs!

speaker: AARON
text: Coal-black is better than another hue

speaker: AARON
text: In that it scorns to bear another hue;

speaker: AARON
text: For all the water in the ocean

speaker: AARON
text: Can never turn the swan’s black legs to white,

speaker: AARON
text: Although she lave them hourly in the flood.

speaker: AARON
text: Tell the Empress from me, I am of age

speaker: AARON
text: To keep mine own, excuse it how she can.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Wilt thou betray thy noble mistress thus?

speaker: AARON
text: My mistress is my mistress, this myself,

speaker: AARON
text: The vigor and the picture of my youth.

speaker: AARON
text: This before all the world do I prefer;

speaker: AARON
text: This maugre all the world will I keep safe,

speaker: AARON
text: Or some of you shall smoke for it in Rome.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: By this our mother is forever shamed.

speaker: CHIRON
text: Rome will despise her for this foul escape.

speaker: NURSE
text: The Emperor in his rage will doom her death.

speaker: CHIRON
text: I blush to think upon this ignomy.

speaker: AARON
text: Why, there’s the privilege your beauty bears.

speaker: AARON
text: Fie, treacherous hue, that will betray with blushing

speaker: AARON
text: The close enacts and counsels of thy heart.

speaker: AARON
text: Here’s a young lad framed of another leer.

speaker: AARON
text: Look how the black slave smiles upon the father,

speaker: AARON
text: As who should say “Old lad, I am thine own.”

speaker: AARON
text: He is your brother, lords, sensibly fed

speaker: AARON
text: Of that self blood that first gave life to you,

speaker: AARON
text: And from that womb where you imprisoned were

speaker: AARON
text: He is enfranchisèd and come to light.

speaker: AARON
text: Nay, he is your brother by the surer side,

speaker: AARON
text: Although my seal be stampèd in his face.

speaker: NURSE
text: Aaron, what shall I say unto the Empress?

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Advise thee, Aaron, what is to be done,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And we will all subscribe to thy advice.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Save thou the child, so we may all be safe.

speaker: AARON
text: Then sit we down, and let us all consult.

speaker: AARON
text: My son and I will have the wind of you.

speaker: AARON
text: Keep there. Now talk at pleasure of your safety.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: How many women saw this child of his?

speaker: AARON
text: Why, so, brave lords! When we join in league,

speaker: AARON
text: I am a lamb; but if you brave the Moor,

speaker: AARON
text: The chafèd boar, the mountain lioness,

speaker: AARON
text: The ocean swells not so as Aaron storms.

speaker: AARON
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Nurse
                .
                 
              But say again, how many saw the child?

speaker: NURSE
text: Cornelia the midwife and myself,

speaker: NURSE
text: And no one else but the delivered Empress.

speaker: AARON
text: The Empress, the midwife, and yourself.

speaker: AARON
text: Two may keep counsel when the third’s away.

speaker: AARON
text: Go to the Empress; tell her this I said.

speaker: AARON
text: “Wheak, wheak”! So cries a pig preparèd to the spit.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: What mean’st thou, Aaron? Wherefore didst thou this?

speaker: AARON
text: O Lord, sir, ’tis a deed of policy.

speaker: AARON
text: Shall she live to betray this guilt of ours,

speaker: AARON
text: A long-tongued babbling gossip? No, lords, no.

speaker: AARON
text: And now be it known to you my full intent:

speaker: AARON
text: Not far one Muliteus my countryman

speaker: AARON
text: His wife but yesternight was brought to bed.

speaker: AARON
text: His child is like to her, fair as you are.

speaker: AARON
text: Go pack with him, and give the mother gold,

speaker: AARON
text: And tell them both the circumstance of all,

speaker: AARON
text: And how by this their child shall be advanced

speaker: AARON
text: And be receivèd for the Emperor’s heir,

speaker: AARON
text: And substituted in the place of mine,

speaker: AARON
text: To calm this tempest whirling in the court;

speaker: AARON
text: And let the Emperor dandle him for his own.

speaker: AARON
text: Hark you, lords, you see I have given her physic,

speaker: AARON
text: And you must needs bestow her funeral.

speaker: AARON
text: The fields are near, and you are gallant grooms.

speaker: AARON
text: This done, see that you take no longer days,

speaker: AARON
text: But send the midwife presently to me.

speaker: AARON
text: The midwife and the nurse well made away,

speaker: AARON
text: Then let the ladies tattle what they please.

speaker: CHIRON
text: Aaron, I see thou wilt not trust the air

speaker: CHIRON
text: With secrets.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: For this care of Tamora,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Herself and hers are highly bound to thee.

speaker: AARON
text: Now to the Goths, as swift as swallow flies,

speaker: AARON
text: There to dispose this treasure in mine arms

speaker: AARON
text: And secretly to greet the Empress’ friends.—

speaker: AARON
text: Come on, you thick-lipped slave, I’ll bear you hence,

speaker: AARON
text: For it is you that puts us to our shifts.

speaker: AARON
text: I’ll make you feed on berries and on roots,

speaker: AARON
text: And feed on curds and whey, and suck the goat,

speaker: AARON
text: And cabin in a cave, and bring you up

speaker: AARON
text: To be a warrior and command a camp.
